data.title: [host project]: hardcoded local DSO repo path used instead of plugin cache -> script access fails on other machines
data.description:
## Incident Overview

A host project session invokes DSO scripts via a hardcoded path to the local DSO repo (/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/scripts/) instead of resolving them through the plugin cache or the .claude/scripts/dso shim.

## Observed Log

Bash command in host project context:
  for id in 88c7-fcb3 e5a7-c242 4efc-bf90 208b-f342 c0ec-9a25; do
    .claude/scripts/dso ticket transition $id open closed 2>&1...
  done
  Error: Exit code 144
  /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-transition.sh: line 118: warning: command substitution: 1 unterminated here-document

The path /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/ is a developer-local absolute path to the DSO plugin source repo. Host projects must NEVER reference this path — they must use the plugin cache (~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/) or the .claude/scripts/dso shim.

## Expected Behavior

Host projects access DSO scripts exclusively via:
1. The .claude/scripts/dso shim (preferred)
2. The plugin cache path (~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/scripts/)

## Actual Behavior

Script resolved to /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-transition.sh — a hardcoded developer-local path that does not exist on other machines or CI.

## Impact

- Breaks portability: any machine that does not have the DSO repo checked out at that exact path will fail
- Bypasses the shim contract and plugin cache isolation
- check-portability.sh pre-commit hook should catch hardcoded paths but this was in a runtime invocation, not a committed file
